name: What is the best flooring for humidity changes in San Francisco Bay Area homes?

mainEntity.name: What flooring is best for homes that go through humidity swings in the Bay Area?
mainEntity.text: What flooring is best for homes that go through humidity swings in the Bay Area?

acceptedAnswer.text: Homes in the Bay Area can experience noticeable humidity changes between coastal fog, rainy periods, and dry indoor heating. Flooring that stays dimensionally stable under those shifts tends to perform best. Engineered wood is often a better wood-look option than solid hardwood because its layered construction helps resist movement. Luxury vinyl plank and tile are even more stable and are widely used in moisture-prone areas. The installer should still check the home’s conditions, because material choice alone does not solve moisture issues. Subfloor dryness, ventilation, crawl space conditions, and acclimation all affect long-term performance. If the floor is installed too soon or over a damp base, even a good product can fail. Ask which materials your contractor recommends for your room type, how they handle moisture testing, and whether the product’s warranty supports installation in your home’s conditions. In variable climates, stability matters more than style alone.
acceptedAnswer.description: Engineered wood, luxury vinyl plank, and tile are usually the most stable options during humidity swings. They are less likely than solid hardwood to expand, contract, cup, or gap when indoor moisture changes seasonally.
